transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hey, Eric.
Happy New Year.
Happy New Year.
How's the...
Oh, it was pretty homebound, to be honest, and I'm still trying to understand what Marty Supreme was, a movie, maybe, I don't know, but how about yourself?
Yeah, it was good.
I was also homebound, but very quiet and relaxing a couple weeks, so good time to recharge.
Excellent.
Yeah, absolutely.
Cool.
Who are we waiting on?
Looks like we've got a quorum with everyone.
Okay.
Cool.
So, yeah, let's just kind of get back up to speed to where we were, if we want to just go through the exception of of
The Airtable, is there stuff that is, there are several ready for our review, we have about 15 or so ready for our review, is that correct?
Go ahead, Jose.
Yeah, I think about 23, those should be ready to be published pretty soon.
I could talk about, like, why we have the backlog in a bit, if you want to know about that, but beyond that, everything should be ready to be published.
If you want me to publish, like, all 23 over the course of one or two days, I can't, but I do feel way more comfortable having that, essentially, a four-week backlog.
Yep.
One of the first mistakes I ever made when I inherited this job was, I was like, why do we have so many articles unpublished?
I published them all, and I was like, oh, we need those margins.
So, I like having that four-week backlog.
Four-week was just the number that was drilled into my head back in the day, and I'm happy to publish, however, whatever amount of these 23 remaining articles you want.
I just loved if we have...
Like, a couple left over.
Okay.

I think if they're done, I mean, we should just space them out, as long as we don't think anything's being too redundant in terms of repeating, but I'm definitely fine spreading it out over four weeks.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
I'll have to take a look at the backlog.
I'm not sure if some of the TTS topics got in there or not, but if we can, like, have some TTS coverage every week.
Then that'd be great.
Yeah, I think that was going to be my point, is really ensuring that we have that.
Cool.
So, yeah, Jose, let me know what you need on that, if I can help.
And then one thing we do, you know, we might think about Jose is, and this is on us, is these cover images, right?

Getting them to be a little bit more, like, reflecting one of the key quotes or something like that, so you and I can chat about that, how we want to ensure think if we do some of the cover images that were suggested in the table, I'll send them to you.
you.
you.
Thank
I would also, I was just curious how these articles publications would coincide with like our announcement article publications because I don't want them to interfere with each other.
No, no, no, no, no.
We clear the airspace for that.
You know, that's like, you know, that's, you know, that's, you know, like a big military flyover.
You don't, you don't mess around with that.
So yeah, yeah, yeah.
We definitely clear space around that.
then let's, let's, Jose, you know, we'll chat later as we, let's just, we should sync on that.
You and I one-on-one, just all the content around the, everything that's going on.
Cool.
Since we weren't able to meet over the holiday and since I'll be out of office during our next bi-weekly, could we sync this week or?
Yes, absolutely.
I'll set up time.
No problem.
Thanks.
Cool.
Now what?
Yeah.
We have our raw assignments.
Yeah.
So can I just, can I just get a little context for these, these assignments on, in the research?
Search Views, where they've come from, and sort of what the process is around these?
me jump to that.
Got too many, there we go, too many tabs open.
So, the ideas for review are the ones that are in the backlog.
And so, typically, we have you guys go through and approve which ones you'd like to go up next, or just like a batch of 10 to 15 gives us a few weeks of leeway.
The voice AI API evaluation is one that we said we wanted to prioritize, given the performance of those, both from a conversion and traffic standpoint, have been doing really well.
So, I wanted to keep that flow and mojo going.
And then you mentioned there's going to be some campaigns or kind of focus on QSS.
This month, and so we had Will do some keyword research to just backfill some topics that we could kind of explore and create content around for QSR.
And then the third priority one that I was going to suggest is enterprise production and deployment.
We only have three articles in that topic cluster published right now.
So I figured it's one that we have a gap in and would like to get some content out there to see how it performs as well.
I'm sorry, this is the enterprise?
Yeah, enterprise production deployment.
Yeah, okay.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
Because the, I believe the 23 that are now in review are all architecture and intelligence and developer implementation and technical guides.
So we'll have plenty of.
Content coming out for those, so I figured these bottom three would be a good priority to start the year off on.
so.
Yeah, I think we definitely want to get the, I like the QSR stuff in there.
That's good.
What we are seeing, though, and I'm looking at, I know you have your system, we have our SCM Rush, and we also have our Dream Data.
I think I've shared that before.
And what's really working is the, has been the competitive stuff that you produce for the most part.

And so I want to be sure that that doesn't, obviously I want to round out on this, but I'd like to, I'd like to come back to some of those and see like where, if there are any gaps or other areas that we can expand.
Because from an SEO and geo perspective, I think the more that we actually just are talking about deepgram and 11 labs or deepgram versus, you know, some of these other players, or I would say.
Unless I've missed it, well, we have it there, like OpenAI, real-time, but also perhaps some of the Claw or, you know, some of the other, Gemini, some of the other LLMs and stuff out there.
So people are searching, like, you know, how do I get a voice agent for my Gemini, you know, agent, something like that.
We have some stuff in there.
Yeah, I was just pulling up that data, but yes, basically what we have seen is that we've tripled our signups month over month from the content you are producing, primarily from the competitive ones.
Yep.
And that's this voice AI API evaluation cluster.
That's where all those live.
Okay.
So, yeah, these are the ones we kind of restocked as of December, and then we just need to get some of these approved.
And we can start developing that content.
Awesome.

So, yeah, whenever, I think, you know, I think, Troy, you have access, and I can get other people's access if you need it, but all you have to do is change it from backlog to approve the start in the status column.
Okay, yeah, yep.
We'll go through that, right, everyone?
So, yeah, so when we look at console sign-up, look at conversions, this is a month in the last six months, we'll go there.
And so, yeah, so, sorry for the high line, but when did we start publishing again?
October?
Yeah, was late September, early October.
Yeah.
So, these are all pages from you, as you can see, these are actually.
Cool signups.
So we started out with, you know, none, we've gone up here, we've gone up here, and it's Whisper versus Deepgram, 11 labs, alternative, and then, you know, some of the other ones.
But, yeah, this is what we're seeing so far.
So all very good work.
Don't think I can point to any SQLs.
Probably not.
It's so early.
But, yeah, very positive results.
As you can see, it's the breakdown here.
Again, yeah, there's none.
That's not the end of the world.
But if we look at the console signups, right, you can see that it is primarily the competitors that are doing it.

So, yeah, I mean, I think anything we can do in terms of, like, and maybe Jose and Hazani got to be a bit more here, we want to get specific around 11 labs as language coverage or language capabilities or stuff like that.
So we're, like, you know, a little bit more nuanced, and maybe we can come up with some ideas around that.
But that's...
I don't want to overdo it, but that's a big takeaway, is that we should continue to look at the competitive space, because I think it's just, obviously, things take a little while to get out there.
We want to still see how stuff is doing.
We can see the benchmark enterprise models explain.
That's up there.
So, yes, definitely enterprise is doing well.
Let's not let the competitive stuff linger, especially since 11 Labs gets a lot more searches than we do.
And I think that, you know, we can probably get some drag off of that.
Yeah, I can certainly start thinking of a next list of topics for around TTS.
We recently were featured in a third-party benchmark for TTS.
That might be a topic to where we've actually ranked pretty well.
Yeah, so that might be a topic worth doing something about.
Right, like, just get, yeah, yeah.
I mean, it's, I'm just from a, you know, 30,000-foot level.
It's like, how do we...
Get more stuff from us, 11 labs, languages, 11 labs, voice, 11 labs, you know, security, you know, all these sort of things, because they are sort of the elephant, the elephant.
So let's, let's lean into that, because we can compete on that.
Yeah, we don't need to, I'm really liking, this is the, this is really, Erik, you know, this is the chart that we can take to, to leadership, and when they say, yeah, this is working, right?
That we are producing content, you know, even though the numbers are, aren't necessarily, um, huge, this is just organic search in LLMs, right?
And we could even, you we wanted to say, what are even LLMs doing?
They're doing anything, haven't gone down.
But yeah, even here, we can point to saying, even though they're just, it's something, you know, we are generating content, it is generating science from LLMs.
We need to keep doing this, but we need to understand, again, Google text-speech comparison, you know.
is doing well where there are 11 labs in text comparison.
So again, it comes at this competitive stuff, and the more granular we can get into that, I think, the more presence we'll have in all this stuff.
Makes sense.
The trajectory is currently on the right track.
Yeah, but I'm very, very happy with how this is going, and I like Jose, you know, putting the brakes on us, and we do have some major news for the company coming up, so let's be cognizant of that.
But let's just keep this data-based approach.
Let's get the backlog flowing and start working on some new competitor content ideas, and that's, I'll leave it at that unless there's something else.
That's good.
The only other thing, Erik, is the language pages.
Yep.
Troy, I'm not sure if you know where we're at with the sanity migration stuff.
I don't know what the precise status is.
I think the aim is at the end.
The most, like, the end of this quarter, I think it should all be done for the most part.
Okay.
I was, I heard the end of last quarter, so I don't know if the goal was.
Well, I think it's, it's, no, it's not, because it's page by, section by section.
Yeah, yeah, Right, so it's like, all of the full, it's like there's one day where it'll go from this to that, so.
Yeah.
It's a little tricky.
I haven't connected with Patricia yet this, this month, so let me sync back with her and find out.
Yeah, I think you're talking, sorry.
Yeah, like, we're, we're all kind of heads down on some stuff until next week, but basically after that, Patricia, I'll, I'll also check in with Patricia specifically on the language page thing.
I'm not sure if you're waiting on her for, for anything.

It was, I think the week before Christmas, she gave us the go-ed that the template was in Sanity, and so I've got the engineering team, they're just working on integrating with Sanity, and then it's kind of the last.
Last step for us before we can start doing bulk publishing of the language pages.
Okay.
So I'm supposed to get an update by the end of this week of their progress on the integration.
So I'm hoping we finish that this week and then by next week we can at least start the publishing process.
Okay, that'd be great.
In the meantime, that covers STT languages.
In the meantime, we're launching new TTS languages.
So I'll start working on the wireframe for that and repeat the same process for the TTS side of things.
Awesome.
Sounds like a plan.
And then the other to-do, more competitive topics, specifically with respect to 11Labs.
So I'll try to ideate on that.
Great.
Awesome.
All right.
Well, thanks, everyone.
We've got plenty.
just checking the backlog of the comparison topic class.
Buster, I don't see any 11 labs, no, Deepgram versus 11 labs versus Amazon poly is the one that we have in there.

But yeah, if we can get any ideas you guys have around kind of 11 labs, kind of long tail keywords we can go after, more than happy to take those and then have those kind of prepared for the backlog as well.
Yeah.
But yeah, things are trending in right direction.
I think, you know, the more content we get out, the more this kind of slowly compounds on itself.
And we'll start to see hopefully the same trajectory month over month with console signups and overall traffic increase.
We saw a big spike in December.
I think it was like a 65% month over month traffic increase.
So things are moving in the right direction.
Yes, absolutely.
Yeah.
Well, Happy New Year, Erik.
Thanks, everyone, for your help.
And, yeah, let's connect again and keep moving forward on this.
But glad we have the data to help us tell us where to go.
Absolutely.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
Bye.
Thanks, Tim.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on content strategy based on recent performance data.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Competitive content is the top priority.** It tripled MoM signups, with "Whisper vs. Deepgram" and "11 Labs alternative" being key drivers.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
  - [**The 23-article backlog will be published over \~4 weeks.** This maintains a strategic buffer and will include weekly TTS coverage.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [**New content will focus on competitive topics.** This includes granular comparisons with 11 Labs and a post on a recent third-party TTS benchmark where Deepgram ranked well.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=812.0)
  - [**The STT language page rollout is imminent.** The Sanity integration is the final blocker, with an update expected this week.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance & Strategic Shift

  - [Data from SEMrush and Dreamdata shows competitive content is the primary driver of new signups.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
      - [**Result:** Console signups tripled MoM since the content program restarted in late September/early October.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)
      - [**Top Performers:** "Whisper vs. Deepgram," "11 Labs alternative," and "Google text-speech comparison."](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=776.0)
  - [This success validates a data-driven approach and justifies a strategic shift to prioritize more competitive content.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0)

### New Content Priorities

  - [**Top Priority → Competitive Content**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
      - [**Focus:** Granular comparisons with 11 Labs (e.g., language coverage, security) to capture search traffic from a larger competitor.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=812.0)
      - [**New Idea:** A post on a recent third-party TTS benchmark where Deepgram performed well.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=872.0)
  - [**Supporting Priorities (for approval)**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=472.0)
      - [**QSR Content:** Backfill topics based on new keyword research.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=534.0)
      - [**Enterprise Production & Deployment:** Expand this cluster, which currently has only three articles.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)

### Publication Plan & Backlog

  - [A 23-article backlog is ready for publication.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [**Decision:** Publish over \~4 weeks to maintain a strategic buffer.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=325.0)
  - [**Cadence:** Include weekly TTS coverage.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=380.0)
  - [**Coordination:** Jose and Troy will sync to avoid conflicts with major company announcement articles.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [**Cover Images:** Jose and Troy will sync on a new strategy to make images more reflective of the article's content.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)

### Language Page Rollout

  - [**STT Language Pages:**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
      - [**Status:** The Sanity template is ready.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
      - [**Blocker:** Engineering is integrating with Sanity; an update is expected by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
      - [**Timeline:** Publishing could begin as early as next week.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1085.0)
  - [**TTS Language Pages:**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1098.0)
      - [**Plan:** Hasan will wireframe a new template for upcoming TTS language launches.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1098.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Jose:**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0)
      - [Schedule 1-on-1 with Troy to coordinate the publication calendar around company announcements.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
      - [Sync with Troy on the new cover image strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [**Hasan:**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1111.0)
      - [Ideate new competitive content topics, focusing on 11 Labs.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1111.0)
      - [Wireframe the new template for TTS language pages.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1098.0)
  - [**Troy:**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=725.0)
      - [Approve new content ideas in Airtable by changing the status to "approved."](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=725.0)
      - [Confirm the final Sanity migration timeline with Patricia.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1009.0)
  - [**Erik:**](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
      - [Provide an EOW update on the Sanity integration for STT language pages.](https://fathom.video/share/iHDt_quyiuYbxUiyx2yqB9b5_PQsoef7?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)

action_items.description: Review backlog; ensure weekly TTS coverage
Send suggested cover images to Troy
Schedule 1:1 w/ Troy re: announcements + content
Approve 10–15 backlog articles in Airtable (set Status=Approved)
Sync w/ Patricia re: Sanity migration status
Check in w/ Patricia re: language pages
Wireframe TTS language pages; then start bulk publish
Ideate next competitive topics vs 11Labs; share w/ Erik
